name: baoyu-danger-x-to-markdown
description: Convert X (Twitter) tweet or article URL to markdown. Uses reverse-engineered X API (private). Requires user consent before use.
capabilities: Convert single tweets to markdown with YAML front matter
Extract full thread conversations with all replies
Parse X Articles into structured markdown format
Authenticate via environment variables or Chrome browser login
Output as JSON or markdown file with configurable paths
author: jimliu
securitySummary: This skill is a legitimate X/Twitter content conversion utility. The heuristic flagged a 'dangerous combination' of code execution, network access, and credential handling. After evaluation, these patterns are FALSE POSITIVES - they represent standard browser automation for X authentication via Chrome DevTools Protocol. The skill uses child_process.spawn to launch Chrome, reads local browser cookies, and makes API calls to X - all required for its documented functionality. The reverse-engineered API usage is transparently disclosed with a consent flow requiring user acceptance before use.
valueStatement: Converting X (Twitter) content to markdown is time-consuming when done manually. This skill automates the extraction of tweets, threads, and articles into clean markdown with YAML front matter, preserving content structure and metadata.
limitations: Uses reverse-engineered X API - may break without notice if X changes
Requires X authentication (auth_token and ct0 cookies)
No official support or guarantees from X
Account restrictions possible if API usage detected by X

useCases.title: Archive Important Tweets
useCases.description: Save tweets and threads from thought leaders, journalists, or public figures to offline markdown files for research and reference.

useCases.title: Blog Content Preparation
useCases.description: Convert X Articles to markdown for republishing or quoting in blog posts with proper attribution metadata.

useCases.title: Knowledge Base Building
useCases.description: Systematically collect and organize Twitter threads on specific topics into a searchable markdown knowledge base.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Tweet Conversion
promptTemplates.prompt:
Convert this X URL to markdown: https://x.com/username/status/1234567890

Save the output to ./output/tweet-1234567890.md
promptTemplates.scenario: Converting a single tweet to markdown

promptTemplates.title: Thread Extraction
promptTemplates.prompt:
Extract the full thread from this tweet URL. The URL is: https://x.com/tech_expert/status/9876543210

Output directory: ./threads/tech-insights/

promptTemplates.title: Article to Markdown
promptTemplates.prompt:
Convert this X Article to markdown format:
https://x.com/i/article/5555555555

Output path: ./articles/topic-name.md

promptTemplates.title: JSON Output for Processing
promptTemplates.prompt:
Convert this tweet URL to JSON format for programmatic processing:
https://x.com/developer/status/1112223333

Use: npx -y bun skills/baoyu-danger-x-to-markdown/scripts/main.ts <url> --json

bestPractices: Set X_AUTH_TOKEN and X_CT0 environment variables for automation without browser login
Use --json flag when integrating with scripts for parseable output
Accept the disclaimer when prompted - the tool uses reverse-engineered X API
antiPatterns: Do not use for bulk scraping - this may trigger X rate limits or account restrictions
Do not share authentication cookies - they grant access to your X account
Do not assume indefinite functionality - reverse-engineered APIs can break without notice

faq.question: How does authentication work?
faq.answer: The skill authenticates via X cookies. Set X_AUTH_TOKEN and X_CT0 environment variables, or the tool will launch Chrome for interactive login on first run.

faq.question: What X content can be converted?
faq.answer: Single tweets, threads (all replies in conversation), and X Articles are supported. Public and authenticated content works.

faq.question: Is this an official X API?
faq.answer: No, this uses a reverse-engineered X API. X may change their API without notice, potentially breaking this tool.

faq.question: Where are cookies stored?
faq.answer: Cookies are cached locally in the skill data directory (~/.local/share/baoyu-skills/x-to-markdown/ on Linux).

faq.question: Can I use this programmatically?
faq.answer: Yes, use --json flag for parseable output. The tool can be called from scripts with URL and output path arguments.

faq.question: What happens if X blocks the API?
faq.answer: The tool may stop working without notice. The disclaimer explains this risk. Always archive important content locally.
